forecast-period.text: A significant low pressure system over waters to the south of SA extends a series of fronts over the coastal waters before moving eastwards and reaching western Bass Strait Tuesday afternoon. A weak low pressure system moves over waters to the south on Wednesday extending a cold front over coastal parts late Wednesday into Thursday. A high pressure system develops over western parts on Friday and a cold front embedded in the southwesterly moves over the coastal waters.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Adelaide Metropolitan Waters
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots.
2 metres.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Tuesday for Adelaide Metropolitan Waters
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots tending westerly in the early morning.
2 to 2.5 metres.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Westerly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the morning.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre around midday.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 metres during the morning.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots tending westerly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
